title: Introduction to Bayesian Statistical Modelling for ecologists
text:
Instructors:
Fitsum Abadi Gebreselassie, New Mexico State University, USA (fgebrese@nmsu.edu)
Jason Marshal, Wits University, South Africa (jason.marshal@wits.ac.za)
Date: 29–31 October 2025
Time: 09h00–16h00
Venue: Wits University, Oppenheimer Life Sciences Building, Room 3034A (Nucleus Lab)
Computer requirements: Please bring your own laptop with the latest versions of R, RStudio, and JAGS installed
Registration: Free
Application: Please use the online form to register. The workshop will be limited to 40 participants.

Workshop Overview
This three-day hands-on workshop aims to introduce ecologists to statistical modelling using Bayesian approaches and BUGS programming. The workshop will cover

a brief introduction to R software and to BUGS programming conventions using the language JAGS;
basic statistical models and their representation in a Bayesian framework, including linear regression, generalized linear models, generalized linear mixed models;
specialised models in population ecology and wildlife conservation, such as occupancy modelling, population modelling, and integrated population modelling.

Prerequisites: Basic knowledge of statistics (i.e. familiarity with simple regression) and some prior exposure to R software. No prior experience with Bayesian statistical modelling is required.
